A big benefit of this new navigation menu is that you can now access your favorite apps directly from any application. Previously, you would have to return to My Home and then click on your favorite app, now you can access it directly from the menu, as shown in Figure 9.

Figure 9: New navigation menu and updated design of notifications and user menu. Alt Text: 3 screen snippets showing each of the menus as a pop-over.

Updated Design of Notifications and User Menu

The new simplified design of notifications shown in Figure 9 fits better with the overall design of SAP IBP, and provides simplier interactions. By default notifications are sorted by date, with grouping by “Today”, “Yesterday”, “Last Week”, “Last Month”. Users can also choose to sort by importance.

The new user menu design shown in Figure 9 provides more space for the avatar of the user, and provides more space between items for easier selection. The settings are always at the top as the first entry, and the “Sign Out” option is separated from the actual navigation options in the list, to make it easier to find.

Launch Apps Directly via Transaction Code

I already covered this new feature in detail in part 2 of my series, since the launchpad is used by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 too. Rather than repeating it here, I suggest that you look at the examples in the corresponding SAP Fiori launchpad subsection of my part 2 blog post:

  • Launch apps directly via transaction code.

Find out more:

  • Documentation: What’s New: Launching an App with its Transaction Code in SAP Fiori Launchpad and Searching and Launching Apps.

Administrators can Upload and Download Spaces

In the Manage Launchpad Spaces app of the SAP Fiori launchpad, administrators can download existing spaces together with their assigned pages and upload them again to another system.

This enables you to transfer spaces and their assigned pages to other systems, for example, when you can't use transports, meaning that you can improve productivity and consistency across systems. Find out more:

  • Documentation: Downloading and Uploading Spaces

 

UX enhancements in applications

We have introduced two very significant innovations that provide a much better user experience, but without dramatically updating the actual UIs! This is because user experience is so much more than just the UI: if a user can get more done than before, with less effort, achieving better ourcomes, then that is a definite improvement in their experience with the system.

The first of these, introduced with the 2508 release, is the harmonized planning area. The second, introduced just now with the 2602 release, is harmonized scenario management. In both cases, the “harmonized” in the name signifies that these new capabilities unify and simplify how users work.

Beyond these, we have introduced a number of user experience enhancements for planning UIs and for master data management, which are covered below too.

Harmonized Planning Area

The harmonized planning area lets you use one planning area to plan both mid- to long-term supply, demand and inventory with time-series buckets and short- to mid-term supply with orders.

Even better, it integrates both live (real-time) and scheduled data flows using the same data model, i.e. attributes, master data types, key figures, planning levels, time profile etc.

With integrated, harmonized tools and processes, planners can smoothly adopt additional SAP IBP capabilities, leading to better reporting and planner efficiency. Improved flexibility in time-series supply planning helps planners quickly adapt to changing demands and market conditions. Centralized application mapping and name space management as well as a new central unit of measure and currency conversion help to streamline configuration. Additionally, planners have on-the-fly access to order key figures, enabling more informed decision-making.

Find out more and watch a demo here:

  • SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) 2508 - Available Now!
  • Getting Started with SAP IBP New Harmonized Planning Area (SAPIBP2)
  • Video: Harmonized Planning Area in SAP IBP – 2508 Release Highlights (5:28 minutes).

Harmonized Scenario Management

Available with the 2602 release, harmonized scenario management in SAP IBP unifies and simplifies how you work with scenarios across orders and key figures, letting you manage the full lifecycle in Planner Workspaces: create, modify, simulate, and promote both order and key figure data within one framework. It extends scenario lifetimes across orders and key figures and adds new promotion options for orders. Controlled shine-through now includes orders, giving you better control of how baseline changes propagate.

Together with the harmonized planning area, it advances integrated planning so orders and forecasts work seamlessly without separate processes. Look at an example and find out more:

  • Video: Harmonized Scenario Management in SAP IBP - 2602 Release Highlight & Demo.
  • Documentation: Harmonized Scenarios.

Planning UI Enhancements

Enhanced Filtering of Workspaces and Segmentation Variants

Introduced with the 2508 release, users can explore data sets more easily now by leveraging segmentation variants. This provides a third level of filtering flexibility for users. The first two options were available previously, the third one is new:

  1. Manually define filter values for the data
  2. Use filter variants to store combinations of filter values for reuse, so that you just choose the variant rather than manually maintaining all the filters.
  3. Define segments using attributes like “Product ID” and “Customer ID” with which you can step through the filtered data from (2).

The new segmentation variants are helpful when a selected filter variant results in a large list of data. Without the segmentation variant, users would still have to do a lot of scrolling. This targeted approach significantly reduces data volume, enhances speed and focus, and delivers a smoother experience to your planning process. Have a look at how it works:

  • Video: Segmentation Variants in Planner Workspaces in SAP IBP - 2508 Release Highlights & Demo. (2:26 minutes)

With the 2602 release, we have further enhanced filtering in the Planner Workspaces app. You can now:

  • Manage planning filters directly in Planner Workspaces, including the options to share, edit, delete, and assign groups to existing filters.
  • Overwrite an existing planning filter when you create a new planning filter with the same name.
  • Use advanced operators to define attribute-based filters in many areas across Planner Workspaces, as well as in URL-based navigations to workbooks and variants.
  • Filter aggregated constraint key figures by the (Not Specified) attribute value.

The advanced operators improve usability significantly: you can use “starts with”, “contains”, “greater than” to quickly get to the data you are interested in. Figure 10 shows an example. You can find our more here:

  • Documentation: Enhancements to Filtering in Planner Workspaces.

Figure 10: Filtering with advanced operators in the Planner Workspaces application. Screenshot of Planner Workspace with filters Time Periods “Next 10 weeks”, Product Group “SMART*” and Location Desc “*DC*”. The list at the bottom shows six entries fulfilling the filter criteria.

Navigate Directly to Root Cause of Planning Obstacle

SAP IBP 2508 enhanced the Planner Workspaces application so that users can navigate directly from an identified issue to the root cause, be it a gating resource or component constraint.

Dedicated planning views provide a clear overview of supply and demand dynamics at these critical bottlenecks, empowering you to make informed adjustments. By encompassing both gating resources and components, this feature accelerates root cause analysis and resolution improving planning efficiency and decision-making. Figure 11 shows an example.

Figure 11: Navigate directly to root cause of planning obstacle within the Planner Workspaces application. Alt Text: Screenshot of Planner Workspace with an open context menu next to a cell in the Demand sheet with a red number. The entry “Show Gating Resources” is highlighted in the menu, with an arrow pointing down to a second sheet at the bottom of the screen showing Capacity.

Easy Scenario Comparison with Scorecards in Planner Workspaces

Scorecards let planners compare what-if scenarios across multiple KPIs to select the best option. Introduced in SAP IBP 2508 and enhanced in 2511, they support up to seven KPIs, each based on a key figure. For each KPI, you can set filters and define a direction: “Bigger is better”, “Smaller is better”, or “Within a range”. Figure 12 shows an example where three scenarios are being compared.

Figure 12: An example scorecard for comparing scenarios in Planner Workspaces. Screenshot of Planner Workspace with a pop-over “Compare Scenarios with Scorecard” showing columns for three scenarios Baseline, Promotions, and Promotions wo AIR, and a list of 7 KPIs for each of them. The baseline column is a different color than the others.

When a key figure appears multiple times, you can rename KPIs for distinct views (e.g., Capacity Supply for Assembly vs. Sub-Assembly). You have a lot of flexibility in how you visualize the comparisons: you can compare scenarios by absolute or percentage differences, and also swap rows and columns. You can also promote the optimal one from the scorecard.

Find out more here:

  • Video: Scorecards in Planner Workspaces in SAP IBP - 2511 Release Highlights & Demo (3:10 minutes).
  • Documentation: Working with Scorecards.

Further Usability Enhancements in Planning UIs

Have a look at the What’s New documentation for further usability enhancements with the 2602 release:

  • Usability Enhancements for SAP IBP, Add-in for Microsoft Excel.
  • Enhancements to Planner Workspaces.

Smarter Master Data Management with New Application

The new version of the Manage Master Data app delivers enhanced functionality with focus on planning area scope and master data relationships. Users can explore comprehensive parent-child connections and drill down into dependencies for thorough impact assessment. The app enables personalized experiences through customizable columns, groupings, and sorting based on parent master data attributes.

Teams can collaborate more effectively by sharing standardized views through public variants, all while benefiting from robust, fine-grained access control through planning area-specific authorizations that ensure security across all operations.

Find out more here:

  • Video: New Manage Master Data App in SAP IBP - 2511 Release Highlights & Demo (1:57 minutes).
  • Documentation: Manage Master Data (New Version).

Collaboration Enhancements (Microsoft Teams)

In addition to the built-in collaboration enhancements mentioned above (for example, in the new master data application), we have invested in real-time collaboration with messaging into Microsoft Teams.

Real-Time Microsoft Teams Messaging for Planners

Planners can now send real-time messages to Microsoft Teams channels directly from SAP IBP when saving key figure data or scheduling application jobs, sharing reason codes, comments, and contextual details. They can select the target Microsoft Teams channel directly, either in the SAP IBP, add-in for Microsoft Excel (see Figure 13), or in the Planner Workspaces application.

This reduces communication delays and ensures transparency across planning teams, especially for those colleagues and stakeholders who aren’t regular SAP IBP users.

Planners also benefit by having less manual effort, since they don’t have to create separate nofifications or e-mails.

Figure 13: Real-time messaging for planners via Microsoft Teams. Alt Text: Two screen snippets: 1) a pop-over for “Save Key Figure Changes”, with a field at the bottom “Share With” containing the entry “Sales and Operations Team/General”. 2) a pop-over showing Microsoft Teams General channel for Sales and Operations Team, with a comment in the thread.

Integration of Microsoft Teams for Jobs

Also new with the 2602 release, you can have jobs automatically post comments to Microsoft Teams channels to indicate when a job step starts running. This ensures that everyone is kept up-to-date in real time, including those colleagues who are not regular users of SAP IBP. Read more here:

  • What’s new documentation: Integration of Microsoft Teams for Jobs.

 

Further information

For information about all the new enhancements for SAP IBP, have a look at these blog posts and the links therein:

  • SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) 2508 - Available Now!
  • SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) 2511 - Available Now!
  • SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) 2602 - Available Now!

 

Continue Reading

I hope you enjoyed this fifth part, covering the new UX innovations available for SAP Integrated Business Planning.

Here's a link to part six, covering exciting new UX innovations available for SAP Fieldglass for managing your contingent workforce:

  • SAP User Experience Q1/2026 Update – Part 6: SAP Fieldglass.

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

entry.title: What’s New in My Home SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 Release?
entry.content:
My Home already serves as a centralized entry point for business users by providing immediate access to key insights, supporting the efficient management of tasks and situations, and enabling users to organize their favorite pages and apps. This helps streamline daily operations and reduces the time spent navigating across the system. The 2602 release builds on this foundation by enhancing how information is delivered, personalized, and consumed—ensuring that users can focus on what matters most.  

The 2602 release of My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition introduces a range of new delivered and beta features, along with meaningful UX enhancements designed to elevate the overall user experience. In this blog, we’ll explore what’s new in this release and how these updates help business users work more efficiently and stay better informed. 

 

Mandatory News – Ensuring critical information is seen by all business users  

With the 2508 release, administrators gained the ability to deliver timely and targeted updates directly to business users through the My Home centralized news feed. The 2602 release takes this a step further by introducing mandatory news articles. News articles containing essential company information can now be marked as required reading. Business users must acknowledge these articles, confirming that they have read and understood the content. This feature plays a critical role in ensuring compliance with legal, safety, and policy-related requirements. It also helps eliminate knowledge gaps by ensuring that all employees receive the same information. During periods of change—such as the rollout of new policies or process updates—mandatory reading ensures that users understand both the reasons for the change and its potential impact, improving alignment across the organization. 

 An example of a news article that is required reading. Alt Text: A screenshot of My Home with a pop-over “Required Reading” that is also highlighted with “Contains Critical News”. The news article has an image at the top, followed by text showing the first part of the article. The “Done” button is greyed out, the “Remind me later” button is visible.

 

The Manage News app has a toggle to mark articles as “Required Reading”. Alt Text: Screenshot of the Manage News app with the toggles set to “Yes”, and some news text in the Description field.

 

News and Pages visibility - A more focused experience 

In the 2602 release we introduced enhanced customization options, allowing business users to control the visibility of news and pages within My Home. This enables users to tailor their workspace according to their role, priorities, and daily responsibilities. By showing only relevant information and tools, My Home becomes more focused and clutter-free, helping users stay productive and work more efficiently without unnecessary distractions. 

My Home with enhanced personalization, showing News deselected and Pages selected. Alt Text: A screenshot of My Home, with eight cards in one row in the Pages section, below the ToDos section showing one situation and the Apps section.

 

My Home Settings pop-over with the new selectors for News and Pages within the News and Pages section. Alt Text: A screenshot showing the Layout part of My Home Settings, where users can select or deselect sections. For the News and Pages section, News is deselected, Pages are selected.

 

Improved Mobile Experience with horizontal scrolling 

For business users on the move, the My Home mobile experience has been enhanced with horizontal scrolling. This update supports visual browsing and improves the discoverability of tasks, apps, and insight cards on smaller screens. As a result, mobile users can navigate content more intuitively and access important information quickly—wherever they are. 

 

AI-assisted Content Addition - Adding apps using natural language. 

My Home enables you to do your job with speed and agility. Even more so now that our 2508 Beta AI – Assisted content addition feature is now available in our 2602 release as a premium feature.  Business users can now add applications using natural language in two clicks. The business user can review and verify the results generated by AI ensuring accuracy.  This saves time by dramatically simplifying the process of adding new applications and tiles to My Home. Now with this new AI assisted feature,  there is no longer a need for business users to discover the appropriate application or even to navigate away from their home page to the application and manually apply filters to find the information they require — thanks to Generative AI, the relevant information can be surfaced quickly and effortlessly using natural language. This significantly reduces the effort needed to discover and add Insights Cards. 

AI-Assisted Smart Personalization of My Home for Applications. Alt Text: The image shows three screenshots: at the top an extract of My Home showing the blue salutation bar, with an AI button “Add Content” on the right. An arrow has been added linking it to the second screenshot, where a popover “Add Content” is shown, where the user has entered “I want to monitor sales orders what app should I use to do this”, and two of four results are listed and visualized: “Track Sales Orders” and “Manage Sales Orders” (shown as an insight tile). Below that a screenshot of the outcome in My Home: the former is added to Apps Favorites, the latter to Insights Tiles.

 

Smart Helpers - Automating Repetitive Tasks 

Business users now have access to the Beta  AI – assisted task automation in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 release. It allows key users and business users to automate their daily activities in SAP’s Fiori apps by using generative AI to create so call “smart helpers”. Those “smart helpers” execute the repetitive activities on behalf of the business user either from the SAP Fiori apps while always keeping the business user in the loop for decisions on data changes.  Business users simply describe their desired business outcome in natural language, related to a specific SAP Fiori elements-based application, and the smart helper will take care of it for you. 

Beta: The planned way of giving users an overview of the AI-assisted task automation smart helpers in My Home. Alt Text: Screenshot of My Home with a section “Smart Helpers” above the “Apps” section, with two tabs “Smart Helpers (4)” and “Active Runs (3)”. The latter is selected, showing three cards with title, description and executed step shown: one is completed, two are running.

 

Beta: Manage Smart Helpers application for defining instructions for the planned AI-assisted task automation. Alt Text: Screenshot showing the instruction text at the top, an empty section for attachments, and the execution plan with four steps: read order requests, analyze request data, simulate pending requests, create sales orders.

 

AI-assisted Smart Solution for situations – Simplifying workflow 

The Beta AI-assisted Smart Solution for Situations in My Home simplifies workflows by dynamically adapting the user interface to guide business users through resolution steps using the power of generative AI. Key information and actions are surfaced though dynamic cards reducing the need for in-depth navigation.  One click resolution of situations directly from the homepage thereby enhancing efficiency. There is support for bank messages for alerts, reaching project end dates when a project stage is not completed and sales quotation expiry situations. 

Beta: AI-Assisted Smart Solution for Situations in My Home. Alt Text: An image with two screenshots: the upper one show the top part of My Home showing five Situation cards in the To-Dos section. One titled “End Date for Project Digital Media Assessment for Media Corp…” is linked with an arrow to an expanded card shown in the second screenshot – the card is taller and wider, contains a summary on the left with two action buttons “Assign To Me” and “Close Situation”, and more details on the right.

 

Final Thoughts 

The My Home 2602 release demonstrates our continued focus on usability, personalization, and effective communication. From mandatory news articles that drive accountability to enhanced customization and mobile usability, these improvements help ensure that My Home remains a powerful and user-centric entry point to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 Beta AI-assisted features such as Beta AI-assisted Smart solution for situations and Smart Helpers are now available for Beta Testing with the 2602 release. At SAP, we’re committed to delivering the best experience possible, which is why we’re inviting you to join our Beta Testing Program. By participating, you’ll get early access to these features, have the chance to share your feedback, and help us fine-tune them before the official launch. Your input will play a key role in shaping the quality and usability of these new capabilities.  

Also, If you are interested in hearing about the latest innovations in SAP Cloud ERP, please join our Public Cloud Practitioner Forum in which SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers share best practices and provide feedback to the SAP development teams about new functionality.

entry.title: How to use Expert Search for SAP Notes & KBAs in SAP for Me
entry.content:
Searching through countless SAP Notes and Knowledge Base Articles (KBAs) can feel overwhelming, especially when you’re looking for something specific. The good news? SAP for Me’s Expert Search feature makes this task much simpler. 

 

Before diving in, let’s clarify what SAP Notes and SAP Knowledge Base Articles (KBAs) are. 

  • SAP Notes describe a software issue and its solution. They include a description of the symptoms, the cause of the error, and the SAP release and support package level in which the error occurs. Depending on the type of error, an SAP Note may also include workarounds and links to support packages that solve the problem, and most SAP Notes contain coding corrections. 
  • SAP Knowledge Base Articles are practical "how-to" guides. Using business language, KBAs answer frequently asked questions arising from your daily work with SAP software. No coding corrections or modifications are provided. 

 

 Why Use Expert Search? 

The “SAP Notes & KBAs Expert Search” in SAP for Me provides an at-a-glance view on relevant updates, helps you organize your favorite notes, and allows you to retrieve notes based on advanced selection criteria, especially as a recurrent task.  This feature is extremely helpful if you carry out regular analyses of relevant notes.

 

Getting Started in 7 Simple Steps:  

  1. Login to SAP for Me with your S-user ID and password. 
  2. From the left-hand dashboard, navigate to Services & Support. 
  3. Click on the KBAs & Notes tab. 
  4. Select the “SAP Notes & KBAs Expert Search” card. 
  5. Click on the “Expert Search” tab.  

     

  6. Use the filter bar to specify your selection criteria. 
    • Example: Components (Starts): BC-DB*.  
    • As you begin typing the component key, the auto-completion feature will assist you in specifying the correct component. Only notes assigned to components that start with the entered key will be displayed. 
  7. Finally, click the “Go” button to run your search.  

 

Note: No special authorization is required in your S-user ID to use the Expert Search function in SAP for Me.  You can apply different filter criteria, such as Search Term, Software Component (Soft Comp), Component (Exact), and more. 

 

For more information, please refer to the Knowledge Base Article (KBA): 2388433- Information about using Expert Search for SAP Notes & KBAs - SAP for Me. 

 

Additional Resources:  

  • Knowledge Base Article (KBA): 2478289 - How to set up notifications for new or updated SAP Notes and/or KBAs with Expert Search filters - SAP for Me 

 

Got More Questions? 

 Please note that the Customer Interaction Center are always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have!    
 

If you have any questions, please feel free to ask us in the Q&A  All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community  

 

Don't Miss Out on Free Webcasts  

We would like to invite you to our free webcasts, which we believe can help you get the best from your support in SAP. You can find all upcoming CIC Webcasts. These sessions are open to all the users and will revolve around hot topics in support today such as SAP for Me, Case Management, etc.  

Additionally, you can explore a range of CIC how-to videos at learning.sap.com. Please feel free to share this information with your colleagues. We are looking forward to your attendance.  

 

Lastly, you may want to access all CIC Blogs here:  https://community.sap.com/t5/tag/CustomerInteractionCenter/tg-p/board-id/crm-blog-sap.  

Thank you for reading!  We hope you find this guide helpful.

entry.title: SAP User Experience Q1/2026 Update – Part 6: SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
This sixth post in my series gives an overview of latest UX innovations in SAP Fieldglass, for managing your external workforce. The design of the supplier home page has been updated, and a number of further design updates have been made for a consistent SAP Business Suite experience. On-boarding is now made easy with WalkMe. Joule supports further use cases now, and also supports multiple languages, not just English. We have some nice embedded AI use cases, creating a better user experience, as well as many improvements in details that make a difference in day-to-day work.

In case you missed it, Part 1 provides general updates about Joule, SAP Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start.

I’ve grouped the UX innovations into these main sections:

  • Updated Supplier Home Page.
  • Design Updates for Suite Consistency.
  • Joule Innovotions.
  • Embedded AI for improved UX.
  • WalkMe for Easy On-Boarding.
  • Selected Usability Improvements in Applications
  • Further Information

 

Redesigned Supplier Home Page

The supplier home page is the first place that suppliers go to when using SAP Fieldglass, and hence it is crucial that it provides the most important information, actions and navigation options. Based on thorough user research, we have now redesigned the page to deliver a more streamlined and user-centered experience. At the same time, the new design also supports our focus on providing a consistent user experience across all products within the SAP Business Suite, as described in the next section.

Figure 1 shows an example. As you can see, the Announcement card is prominently visible. Users can choose to close it, in which case a button appears allowing them to re-open. The short video in Figure 2 shows what this looks like on a different example page.

You can configure the layout of the redesigned home page for each role with the Layout Manager, which was introduced in the May 2025 release. This learning journey tells you how to do it.

Figure 1: The redesigned supplier home page in SAP Fieldglass. Alt Text: Screen image showing a welcome banner at the top, and a collapsed vertical navigation bar running down the left side. The main screen has a section with pinned links, below which a selection of various cards are shown such as a list of My Work Items, Active Job Postings, Announcements. Two cards have an illustrated message that they have no data to display.

Figure 2: Hiding and re-opening the Announcement card. Alt Text: a short video showing the top half of the home page and the user clicking on “Close” and then “View Announcement”.

Find out more:

  • Smooth and Superior: The New, User-Centered Supplier Home Page.
  • Documentation: Redesigned Supplier Home Page.

 

Design Updates for Suite Consistency

We have made design improvements which are relevant for a consistent user experience across the entire SAP Business Suite, and which are now also available with SAP Fieldglass. As I mentioned in my April blog post Upcoming Design Updates for the SAP Business Suite (SAP Fiori for Web), consistency of the user experience across the various applications used by a user is an important part of overall usability, helping users to feel confident and at ease when using a suite of products – no unexpected surprises.

In this section, we’ll look at these updates (the first two cover the design updates for consistency):

  • Shell bar, navigation menu, user menu.
  • Redesigned user settings.
  • Design updates for more consistent applications.

Shell bar, navigation menu, user menu

Figure 1 above already features the new shell bar, with the collapsed side navigation bar on the left, showing a vertical list of icons representing menu entries. Users can expand the menu to better understand what each menu icon represents, as shown in Figure 3. This is done by clicking on the icon in the top left corner to the left of the SAP logo. It stays expanded until the user closes it, by clicking on the same icon again.

Figure 3 also shows the redesigned user menu, which follows the design for all SAP Business Suite products.

Figure 3: Navigation menu expanded, new user menu. Alt Text: screenshot of the home pages with the navigation menu on the left showing titles next to the icons, the user menu showing two entries “Settings” and “Legal”, and a separate “Sign Out” option at the bottom.

Redesigned User Settings

Selecting Settings in the user menu shown in Figure 3 above brings you to the redesigned settings page, shown in Figure 4 below. This design follows the SAP Fiori design guidelines, thus providing additional UX consistency across the suite. Beyond these visual aspects, it consolidates fields from the My Profile page and the My Preferences page into a single unified Settings page, which is easily accessed via the user menu. Some of the categories have been renamed too. Find out more.

  • Documentation: Updates to Settings Page.

Figure 4: Redesigned and consolidated user settings. Alt Text: Screenshot showing setttings, with full width cards “User Information” and “Appearance”, each with an “Edit” button top right.

Design Updates for More Consistent Applications.

SAP Fieldglass has also introduced the new illustrations from the SAP Design System (SAP Fiori). You can see some examples in Figures 1 and 2 above.

In addition to that, we have also aligned the design of individual applications with the design system. For example, the decision form list view now has the filters at the top of the page, and the Apply Filter button has been renamed to Go, thus aligning terminology – see Figure 5.

Figure 5: Decision form list view filters and Go button. Alt Text: Screen snippet showing “Decision Forms” above two rows of six and three filters. On the far right a highlighted “Go” button.

We have also moved the icons for table actions to the top of the decision form table, again for more consistency. Find out more in the documentation:

  • Decision Form List View UI Updates.

We have made similar updates in other applications, such as the project list view. You can find the full list in the What’s New documentaiton:

  • Project List View User Interface Updates
  • What’s New Viewer for SAP Fieldglass.

 

Joule innovotions

Now, you can use Joule:

  • in many languages, not just English;
  • to recommend job posting templates when create job postings;
  • to approve decision forms, including legal entity recognition.

Joule: Multi-Language Support

You can use Joule in all the languages available in SAP Fieldglass execept for Mongolian, which is currently not available in Joule. Figure 6 shows an example using French, which is used in many countries, not just France.

Figure 6: Joule multi-language support, showing an example in French. Alt Text: Three Joule images starting with a list of suggested prompts, the user chose “Créer une offre d’emploi”, then showing how Joule asks for details, the user entered “chef de projet”, Joule offered three templates, the user chose one and Joule shows the details. In the background a screenshot showing user preferences with the language French selected.

Not only can you interact with Joule in your native tongue, you can also use Joule to find job posting templates and statement of work templates that your company has translated into your language.

For companies that translate job postings and statement of work templates into multiple languages, Joule can automatically detect the user's preferred language and retrieve the corresponding localized template, if available. If not available in the user's preferred language, templates in the company-defined language will be returned. Find out more:

  • Documentation: Translated Template Retrieval in Joule.

Joule: Job Posting Template Recommendations

If you need to create a job posting, Joule can make this easier for you: after stating your requirements for the job, Joule will recommend the top three job posting templates that match best. Figure 7 shows an example where the user has asked for a “Project Manager with 3+ years experience”. In this example, Joule is in full-screen mode, giving space for details of a selected template to be shown on the right. The example shows that the user has clicked on the first suggestion in the list on the left.

Figure 7: Using Joule for SAP Fielglass to create a job posting for a contingent worker, with Joule providing top 3 matching job posting templates. Alt Text: Screenshot of Joule in full-screen mode, with prompts and outcome as described in the text.

Joule: Legal Entity Recognition

If Legal Entity functionality has been enabled in your SAP Fieldglass system, so that you can manage the external workforce for more than one company, you can now specify the legal entity as part of your requirement to Joule when creating job postings or statements of work. Figure 8 shows an example for creating a job posting for the legal entity “UK”.

Figure 8: Legal Entity Recognition by Joule for SAP Fieldglass when creating a job posting. Alt Text: Four Joule images, from left to right, with prompt “Create Job Posting”, Joule asks for details, then “programmer, legal entity is UK”, Joule shows 3 templates, user chose one, Joule asks to confirm, user says “yes”, Joule shows details for reviewing before creating.

Read more:

  • Documentation: Legal Entity Recognition in Joule.

Joule: Decision Form Approvals

When asking Joule for pending work items, Joule will now include Decision Form approvals too, as shown in Figure 9.

Figure 9: Joule for approving Decision Forms. Alt Text: Five Joule screenshots, user chooses “View my pending items”, Joule shows one “Contractor Vetting” pending approval, user selects “Approve Decision Form” from the context menu, Joule asks for a comment, user says “no comment”, decision form is approved.

More Information on Joule

Read this blog post and watch the video to learn more:

  • What’s New for Joule in SAP Fieldglass – November 2025.
  • Video: Introducing Joule in SAP Fieldglass (3:30 minutes).
  • Video: SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - Joule (5:17 minutes).

 

Embedded AI for improved UX

AI-Assisted Skills-Based Hiring

When looking to hire consultants or contingent workers, you want to ensure that you get people with the right skills. Now, SAP Fieldglass makes this easy, with AI assistance.

AI helps you to specify which qualifications to ask for when creating a new job posting: AI-powered qualification recommendations uses AI to analyze your job title and description and then suggests relevant skills. The screen on the left in Figure 9 shows what this looks like.

AI helps you to consolidate qualifications that have different names for the same qualification. Suppliers describe the skills of their employees themselves, leading to a many different names for essentially the same skill. For example, “Azure Cloud,” “Azure”, and “MS Azure” could be used for someone with skills with Microsoft Azure Cloud. With the help of AI, these qualifications can be consolidated into one unique qualification. The screen top right of Figure 10 shows an example of how this is done.

Once skills have unique names, the other aspect that needs to be harmonized is the rating scale: some suppliers use 1 to 5, others perhaps 1 to 6 or 1 to 10. SAP Fieldglass normalizes these.

Having created a posting with appropriate skills, SAP Fieldglass helps you select candidates by making proposals based on the skills (and their ratings) that match, in addition to proposing candidates that have the appropriate job code. The image in Figure 10, bottom right, shows an example.

Figure 10: AI-powered qualification recommendations, qualification consolidation and skill-based candidate proposals in SAP Fieldglass. Alt Text: Three screen snippets. On the left a list of 6 recommended qualifications for skills such as Excel, Powerpoint, Word, with a relevancy shown as a percentage (55%, 50%, …). Top right a list showing Qualification “Microsoft Azure” as heading, with two entries below “Azure Cloud” and “MS Azure” and buttons “Consolidate Qualifications” and “Cancel”. Bottom right a list of 4 potential candidates, their names greyed out, with the qualification matching percentage shown in the far right column.

Read more in this blog post:

  • Smarter Skills-Based Hiring with SAP Fieldglass

Automated Approvals for Resource Time Sheets

Another example where AI can make users much happier is approving resource time sheets. In industries that have high volumes of worker time submissions, manually and dealing with discrepencies in the submitted time sheets can be tedious and time consuming. Now, you can define tolerance limits so that hours that fall within the defined tolerances are automatically approved. This reduces manual effort significantly, freeing up users to do more value-adding activities.

Read more about it:

  • Documentation: Automated Approvals for Resource Time sheets Leveraging Tolerance Limits.

 

WalkMe for Easy On-Boarding

First-time users can often feel a bit lost when using new business software, no matter how easy it is to use – simply the fact that it is unfamiliar can make people feel uncomfortable.

This is where WalkMe comes in, helping users discover how to use the product. SAP Fieldglass now provides WalkMe content out of the box. Figure 11 shows how users can choose from a list of topics. Figure 12 shows how it looks like if a user starts a topic (the welcome pop-over shown on the left), and how WalkMe gives users guidance for each step in the process (the image on the right).

Figure 11: Starting WalkMe in SAP Fieldglass. Alt Text: Screenshot of the home page with a full-height pop-over on the right with heading “Type in your question” above a text entry field, below that sections “General”, “Contingent Workforce”, “Services” with a list of entries such as “Review work items” or “Create a job posting”.

Figure 12: WalkMe examples in SAP Fieldglass. Alt Text: Two screenshots with pop-overs. On the left: “Review work items” with a description and a “Guide me” button; on the right “Approve Time Sheet” pop-over with a small popover connected to the “Approve” button, explaining what to do.

Find out more:

  • Documentation: WalkMe Walkthroughs Available in SAP Fieldglass.

 

Selected Usability Improvements in Applications

Applications in SAP Fieldglass are constantly improving, in many cases not just with new features and functions but also with aspects that improve the overall user experience. Even if they are only improvements in details: the details matter if you want to provide a good experience, since if you get them wrong, they can be very annoying.

Below are some highlights for these improvements in details.

Reusable Resource Types for faster resource creation
Rather than having to always manually enter all the data for a new contractor or piece of equipment, you can define those entries that you want to have pre-filled, by defining reusable resource types. For example you can define address, job code, time tracking, work schedule, activity checklist and more just once, and then use this to pre-fill your fields when creating a new resource. Find out more here:

  • Standardize Resource Creation with Reusable Resource Types in SAP Fieldglass

Tailored list views based on user roles
List views are core to daily operations for teams managing job postings, work orders, and statements of work. However, only some of the many columns shown are relevant to a user in specific role. Although users could customize their views, it turns out that this is rarely done. Now, customers can define views per role, specifying which columns to show and which to hide. This supports faster, more focused decisions and delivers a cleaner, more intuitive experience. Find out more:

  • Documentation: Customized List Views.

One-click line item copy for purchase orders
Here is a nice little detail that will make users smile: with one click, you can now copy the previous line item when creating a purchase order. In many procurement scenarios the same services are purchased regularly, so it is very helpful for users to copy an item and then make only the few necessary changes before continuing with the next item. As you can see in Figure 13, we have introduced a “copy” icon to duplicate the existing PO line.

Figure 13: One-click duplication of purchase order line. Alt Text: a screen snippet showing a PO line with the icon for copying the line on the left of the line.

Making it easier for suppliers to manage line items
We have introduced a number of improvements for suppliers working on SoWs (Statements of Work). For example, when purchasing contracts are in place, it makes sense to include information from the contract in a purchase order to ensure compliance, accurate pricing and so on. Navigating to the right information in the contract when creating a purchase order has been a challenge – but no longer! Suppliers can search and insert contract segments in any order now. Find out more:

  • Documentation: Supplier Workflow Enhancements on SOW Characteristics Tab.

Making it easier for suppliers to maintain charges against PO items
In asset-intensive and plant maintenance industries, suppliers frequently need to submit or resubmit charges against PO lines. This is especially common when working with complex SOWs that involve multiple tasks and services. Up until now, suppliers encountered multiple navigation paths when adding or correcting line items, particularly after a charge is rejected due to issues like incorrect pricing or missing details. Now, this has been made significantly easier: all relevant actions are accessible no matter where they begin the process. Figure 14 shows an example.

Figure 14: Supplier access to all actions when managing PO line charges. Alt Text: two screenshots, showing the card “Corrective maintenance repairs – Engineering Repairs Services and Parts” in different contexts, on the left with an “Edit” button, on the right with an “Itemize” button.

 

Further Information

For an overview of the major new features with the 2511 release, have a look at:

  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Spotlight Features Guide.

These videos are available too:

  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - Joule
  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - Configuration Manager
  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - General Updates
  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - Services Procurement

Have a look at all the latest updates, month by month, in the

  • What’s New Viewer for SAP Fieldglass.
  • This blog post explains how to use the What’s New Viewer:
    SAP Fieldglass November 2025 Release Communication Now Available!

Another good place to stay up to date is the SAP Fieldglass page on SAP Community.

 

Continue Reading

I hope you enjoyed this sixth part, covering SAP Fieldglass. Here's a link to part seven next, covering latest UX innovations for SAP Analytics Cloud:

  • SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 7: SAP Analytics Cloud.

Part 1 of the series lists all the parts, and links to those parts that have already been published:

  • SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 1: AI, Joule, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Mobile Start.

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

For general information on design at SAP and SAP Fiori, check out:

  • www.sap.com/design.
  • www.sap.com/fiori    
  • SAP Community: SAP Fiori topic page.

entry.title: Customize SAPUI5 apps for business users – make an impact by joining our CEI project
entry.content:
SAP Customer Engagement Initiatives (CEI) on https://influence.sap.com allow you, as an SAP customer or partner, to gain early insights into SAP's product developments and work directly with SAP teams to shape future product directions. This project presents a unique opportunity to work with SAPUI5 flexibility product managers and engineering teams, leveraging your expertise customizing SAPUI5 apps with key user adaptation.

Join the upcoming CEI project to make an impact on how key users interact with key user adaptation

If you’re already convinced – join here. If not, read on 🙂

What is key user adaptation?

Key user adaptation is a mode that allows extending SAPUI5 applications for other users right in the browser. It is highly intuitive and requires no technical or programming skills. Just use drag and drop to make UI changes – no modification or copy of SAP standard coding necessary! Read more here.

What is the targeted improvement?

Although the product is mature and widely used, we actively evolve it by delivering new functionality and capability enhancements alongside continuous user experience improvements, informed by UI testing and direct customer feedback. 

We are in the process of overhauling key user adaptation’s UI, simplifying the overall approach while making often-used scenarios more streamlined. We’ve taken a close look at the version history (of activated adaptations) and how our users interact with it, extended the “embed third-party content” feature, widened the scope of custom fields that can be added to the UI – and more.

To ensure that we are on the right way, we need your help!

Who should participate?

Users who want to adapt standard SAPUI5 apps for business users. Both key users and technical users are welcome.

Why should you participate?

  • Gain understanding of SAPUI5 flexibility
  • Chance to influence a central SAPUI5 feature
  • Early access to prototypes

How can you participate?

Don’t miss out on this opportunity to share your feedback and engage with SAPUI5 flexibility development teams. Register by March 15, 2026, and make a true impact on how you can customize SAP apps to your needs!

Click here to sign up for our project. (A short overview about how to get started on influence.sap.com can be found here).

After you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call where you will get more information. This call is followed by individual or grouped feedback sessions.

Thank you in advance for helping us shape the future of SAPUI5 flexibility!

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA Cloud：增强版 New User Experience Switch 全面解析
entry.content:
在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中，SAP 持续推进用户体验（UX）的革新，其中 New User Experience Switch（新版用户体验切换功能） 是帮助企业顺利完成应用替换的重要机制。最新版本（2602）对该功能进行了进一步增强，让业务用户与管理员更容易适应新旧应用的切换进程。 

         本文将带你了解这个增强功能的核心设计、工作方式，以及企业在实际使用中可以获得的价值。

一、为什么需要 New User Experience Switch？

         SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 每 6 个月发布一次新版本，其中会逐步引入新应用（phase-in），同时也可能逐步淘汰旧应用（phase-out）。通常，业务用户有 至少 12 个月 的窗口期来适应新的替代应用，但在实际项目中，这段时间可能转瞬即逝。 

         为避免用户错过迁移时机，SAP 引入 New User Experience Switch，通过界面提示与切换选项来“温和推动”用户尝试并过渡到新应用。

二、New User Experience Switch 的三种提示阶段

         切换功能在应用生命周期的不同阶段会呈现不同的提示样式：

1. Information 信息阶段

  • 告诉用户有新的应用版本正在被引入
  • 展示新应用的优势
  • 鼓励用户尝试
  • 用户可以随时切回旧应用

         这是“最温和”的阶段，旨在减少用户对改变的抵触情绪。

2. Warning 警告阶段

         当旧应用已经被标记为 deprecated（即将淘汰） 时，切换功能会显示黄色的警告信息：

  • 告知旧应用将在未来版本中不再提供
  • 提醒用户尽快完成迁移

3. Alert 紧急阶段

         这是旧应用被移除前的最后一个版本，切换功能会以更醒目的方式提示：

  • 强调迁移的紧迫性
  • 显示明确的移除时间点

         在这个阶段，如果用户仍然停留在旧应用上，会面临无法继续使用的风险。

三、切换功能如何呈现？

         SAP 在 2502 版本引入 New User Experience Switch，在 2508 与 2602 的版本中进一步增强：

  • 旧应用启动时出现提示 popover（气泡提示）
  • Shell Bar（顶端工具栏）中出现 切换按钮
  • 用户可在新旧应用之间自由切换（取决于阶段）

         这些交互方式让用户无需离开当前体验即可了解版本状态并快速做出选择。

四、对业务用户意味着什么？

✔ 更轻松的学习曲线

不再需要由管理员单独告知新旧应用替代关系，系统会自动提示。

✔ 提前感知变化

避免新版本发布后突然发现旧应用“消失”，有效减少工作中断。

✔ 自主试用新应用

在 phase-in 阶段，用户可以自由在新旧体验中切换，加速熟悉度提升。

五、对管理员意味着什么？

管理员可以利用此功能：

  • 提前规划应用替换节奏
  • 知道哪些用户仍在使用旧应用
  • 在企业层面推动统一迁移

这让应用治理更加可控，也能减少升级周期带来的沟通成本。

六、为什么说这个功能“让改变更容易”？

New User Experience Switch 正是帮助企业完成改变的机制：

  • 让改变更加可见
  • 让迁移更加自主
  • 让用户更有掌控感

对于每一个正在推进数字化、云化的企业来说，这种设计都能有效降低内部阻力。

结语：更智慧、更温和的用户体验演进机制

         New User Experience Switch 的增强不仅让用户更轻松地迈向 SAP Fiori 的现代化体验，也让管理员和企业能够更从容、可控地管理应用生命周期。

         随着 SAP 持续对 UX 的改进（例如 2602 版本中的 AI 辅助、增强的 Launchpad、手机端优化等），未来企业的 SAP 使用体验将更加智能、一致、顺滑。 

         如果你正在规划 S/4HANA Cloud 的上云旅程或准备迎接新版本，这项功能值得你重点关注。

 

参考文档：SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA Cloud – New User Experie... - SAP Community

entry.title: How Do I Activate a Situation Type in the Extended Framework?
entry.content:
Situation Handling notifies users about issues requiring their attention. You may choose SAP delivered situation templates or design your own use cases. For the extended framework for Situation Handling, we often discussed how you can create custom situation templates. The framework comes also with SAP delivered use cases that you can easily activate.

This blog post explains how you can activate situation types from SAP delivered situation templates with the extended framework for Situation Handling. It has been available since SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2202 and SAP S/4HANA 2021 FPS02.

 

SAP Delivered Use Cases

Looking for Situation Handling use cases? Visit SAP Help and navigate to Situation Handling, then choose Use Cases. You can narrow your results by filtering for Situation Template Type (either extended object-based or message-based), area, and topic. To learn more about a specific use case, simply check the link in the Situation Template column for a detailed description.

Use Case

For illustration purposes we use the situation template Expiration of Purchase Contracts (PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR). Purchasers get a heads-up for purchase contracts that are about to expire.

 

Activation Overview for 2SL and 3SL

Before discussing the activation steps in detail, let me point out the differences between system landscapes and their specific transport paths.

In the extended framework for Situation Handling, you create a situation type from a situation template. Situation templates are only visible in the quality system of a 2-system landscape (2SL) and the development system of a  3-system-landscape (3SL). The situation type needs to be transported to the production system. The transport paths differ between 2SL and 3SL.

Transport the Situation Type in 2SL

In a 2-system-landscape, you transport the situation type from the quality system to the production system.

  • In the quality system you
    • Create a situation type from a situation template
    • Test if the configuration alerts the right users about the situation
    • Export the well-defined situation type
  • In the production system you
    • Import the situation type
    • Since the data usually differs between quality and production system you need to enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Enable the situation type

Transports in 2-System-Landscape

Transport the Situation Type in 3SL

In a 3-system-landscape, transports are required from the development system to both the test and production system. In a 3SL environment you may also have different tenants for development, customizing, and parallel customizing that need to be considered as well.

  • On the customizing tenant in the development system you
    • Create a situation type from a situation template
    • Export the situation type
  • On the test tenant in the test system you
    • Import the situation type
    • Since the data usually differs between systems you need to enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Test if the configuration alerts the right users about the situation
    • Forward the well-defined situation type
  • On the production tenant in the production system you
    • Import the forwarded situation type
    • Enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Enable the situation type

Transports in 3-System-Landscape

Detailed Activation of a Situation Type in 3SL

Now let’s check the detailed steps for a sample use case in a 3-system-landscape as it is more comprehensive. In 2SL the export / import mechanism resembles the one from development system to test system in 3SL.

Depending on your system setting, the transports may also be processed automatically. For optimal clarity, the blog post discusses the transport steps through manual triggers.

Development System

Create a Situation Type from a Situation Template

Log-in as business process configuration expert on the customizing tenant in the development system. Open the Manage Situation Types – Extended app where you see a list of SAP delivered templates. Use the search option to find the template for our use case Expiration of Purchase Contracts (PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR). Select the template and an object page opens. In the header, select Create Situation Type.

Create situation type from situation template

A new page opens with the situation type that contains all information from the situation template. You just need to enter an ID, Name and Description. The type is configured and ready to use. Depending on your requirements you could adapt the settings, for example, by adding filter values for the conditions, changing texts or rescheduling the batch job.

I recommend monitoring the instances to see how situations are handled.

Monitor situations

Let’s check the relevant settings and understand how the configuration works.

There are two Situation Triggers that detect situations and cover different usage scenarios. While the batch-based trigger checks for situations in the background, the event-based trigger reacts on every active change of the contract.

Situation triggers

The situation triggers cover the following cases:

  • The Event-Based Trigger checks the conditions whenever you create or update a purchase contract.
    • Condition 1 checks if the validity period of a purchase contract expires within the next 30 days and triggers a situation. The information displayed to the user is defined in Situation Display number 1.
    • Condition 2 is only executed if condition 1 did not match. That is, the validity period is not within the next 30 days, for instance when the contract got extended, and the situation is closed. As nobody will be informed about the closure, the texts are empty (Situation Display number 2).

Event-based trigger

  • In addition to the events, the Batch-Based Trigger regularly checks the validity end date according to the batch schedule.
    • Condition 1 checks if the validity period of a purchase contract expires within the next 30 days and triggers a situation.

Batch-based trigger

The configuration for the Batch Job Scheduling specifies that the contract dates are verified every 3 hours throughout the day, beginning at 6:00 a.m.

Batch job scheduling

Export the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator on the customizing tenant in the development system. Open the Export Software Collection app.

  • On top of the table choose Create Software Collection and a new page opens
  • Choose Add Items and select the situation type you created
  • On the header select Check and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Export and wait until the export is finished successfully

Test System

Import the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator on the test tenant in the test system. Open the Import Collection app.

  • Choose your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Manual Import and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Import on the bottom and wait until the import is finished successfully

Test the Situation Type

As business process specialist you can test the situation type on the corresponding tenant.

Make sure that the situation type is enabled.

Activate the situation type

Then maintain the conditions. The filter values are not transported since they may differ in the various systems and tenants.

  • Select the Event-Based Trigger and a new page opens
    • Condition 1 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
      • Validity Period End: enter Next30Days, using the value help
    • Condition 2 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
    • Go back to the main page

Event-based trigger

  • Select the Batch-Based Trigger and a new page opens
    • Condition 1 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
      • Validity Period End: enter Next30Days, using the value help
    • Go back to the main page

Batch-based trigger

With the situation triggers, you defined when a situation is detected. Now, you need to define who should be informed about situations. Maintain the team(s) in the Manage Teams and Responsibilities app and ensure that at least one team is enabled. The filter settings work the same way as with the standard framework for Situation Handling.

Situation recipients

Now you can test the situation type in your business area with the corresponding application data. You can check if there are purchase contracts that match the conditions by choosing Trigger Batch and Manage Instances.

Test the type with trigger batch

If no situations were detected you can log-in as purchaser and create or manipulate a purchasing contract. Add an end date within the next 30 days and the save event will trigger a situation.

When you log-in as a team member of the recipients team you can also check, if you were alerted about the situation.

After the successful test you push the situation type to the production system.

Forward the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator and open the Import Collection app.

  • Select your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Forward and wait until the validation is processed successfully
  • Then choose Forward on the bottom and wait until the export is finished successfully

Production System

Import Collection to Production System

As administrator open the Import Collection app on the production tenant in the production system.

  • Choose your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Manual Import and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Import on the bottom and wait until the import is finished successfully

Activate the Situation Type

Log-in as business process specialist and open the Manage Situation Types – Extended app. Like in the test system, you need to maintain the situation type.

  • Enable the situation type
  • Add the filter values for the trigger conditions
  • Ensure that at least one recipient team is enabled

Now you are all set.

Further Information

You find many more blog posts in the Best Practices section of Intelligent Situation Handling in the SAP Community. And you can visit SAP Help Portal for further guidance on Situation Handling.

entry.title: SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 7: SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud has invested a lot in User Experience, to make it smooth, intuitive and joyful. It supports SAP Business Suite consistency, with the Horizon theme and further updates for navigation, search and more, making it look better and be more intuitive. Users also benefit from the updated home page, AI-assistance, the new My Metrics for staying up-to-date, as well as updates to charts and tables. Story designers also benefit from numerous enhancements to help them produce better content. Finally, we have also made UX enhancements for administrators.

This series of blog posts covers innovations in user experience in our public cloud products from the last six months or so. In case you missed it, Part 1 provides general updates about Joule, SAP Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start, along with links to all the other parts.

I’ve grouped the main UX innovations in SAP Analytics Cloud into these sections:

  • Updated home page
  • Design updates for suite consistency
  • AI-assistance
  • Personalizing your experience
  • Chart and table enhancements
  • Helping designers produce better content
  • Enhancements for administrators
  • Further information

 

Updated Home Page

The home page is the first place that users go to when using SAP Analytics Cloud, and hence it is crucial that it provides the most important information, actions and navigation options. Based on thorough user research, we have now redesigned the page to deliver a more streamlined and user-centered experience. At the same time, the new design also supports our focus on providing a consistent user experience across all products within the SAP Business Suite, as described in the next section.

Figure 1 shows an example.

Figure 1: The redesigned home page in SAP Analytics Cloud. Alt Text: screenshot showing a welcome message “Hello, William Yu” at the top, above a page with 7 cards: one filling a quarter of the screen showing learning news, one showing “Just Ask”, one with four quick actions, and four vertical tiles showing lists of files and resources. The left side has a collapsed vertical navigation menu with icons.

Resizable Cards

You can now resize homepage cards vertically, horizontally, or both (depending on card type). For example, you could choose to make the “Learn about SAP Analytics Cloud" card shown in Figure 1 smaller, as depicted in Figure 2.

The ability to resize cards allows you to personalize the home page to suite your priorities and your way of working.

Figure 2: Resizable cards: Alt Text: On the left a wide card “Learn about SAP Analytics Cloud” is shown, with two columns each with text, image and action button, above a section with a link and a search field for documentation. On the right a card “Learn” half as wide, with a carousel for the columns, showing only one column, above the search field.

Cards for Custom Messages and Announcements

Administrators can create custom cards for providing information to all users, such as announcements on upgrades, maintenance windows, or for promoting key content, such as links to training, process updates or new company guidance. These cards can include images, text, tags, and links. Figure 3 shows an example:

Figure 3: Custom card for informing users. Alt Text: A card with an image above a title and two paragraphs of text, followed by two links to more information.

Design Updates for Usability and Suite Consistency

We have made design improvements which are relevant for a consistent user experience across the entire SAP Business Suite, and which are now also available with SAP Analytics Cloud. As I mentioned in my April 2025 blog post Upcoming Design Updates for the SAP Business Suite (SAP Fiori for Web), consistency of the user experience across the various applications used by a user is an important part of overall usability, helping users to feel confident and at ease when using a suite of products – no unexpected surprises.

In this section, we’ll look at these updates:

  • Horizon theme.
  • Further usability improvements (shell bar, navigation menu, user menu, settings, search, illustrations).

Horizon Theme

The visual theme defines the “look” of a product, so I’m glad to say that SAP Analytics Cloud now also provides the Horizon Theme, as shown above in Figure 1. Administrators can switch it on; soon, it will become the default theme. Not only is Morning Horizon available: you will have access to Morning Horizon and Evening Horizon (the light and dark themes) as well as to the high contrast white and black themes that are available for accessibility.

Users can choose which theme they want to use via the new settings UI, as shown in Figure 4 below.

Further Usability Improvements

Shell bar: Figure 1 above already features the new shell bar at the top of the screen, with the product name to the right of the SAP logo.

Navigation: The redesigned navigation bar can be seen on the left side, showing a vertical list of icons representing menu entries for content and tools. Users can hover over each icon to see the name of the module or expand the menu to see all the names shown next to the icons.

User menu (user profile): Figure 4 shows the redesigned user menu. This design is consistent across the SAP Business Suite, with Settings as the first entry, and Sign-Out clearly separated from the other entries.

Unified Settings for Home Page and User Profile: The Homepage settings and User Profile settings are now consolidated and unified in one intuitive interface, which follows the design for consistency across the suite. No need to go to different menus to adjust your personal preferences and homepage layout anymore. Selecting Settings in the user menu brings you to the redesigned settings pop-over. Figure 4 above shows the settings for Appearance, where the user can select the visual theme they would like to use, and Figure 5 below shows the settings for the home screen.

Figure 5: Home Screen settings in the new consolidated settings UI. Alt Text: Screenshot showing the Settings pop-over, with Home Screen selected in the list on the left, and selectors on the right for cards and tools to display on your home screen.

Search: Search now features auto-complete, making it noticeably more convenient and user-friendly. As you begin typing, relevant suggestions appear instantly, streamlining the process of finding models, stories, and files. This enhancement helps you locate what you need more efficiently, even if you can’t recall exact names, ensuring a smoother search experience.

Illustrations: SAP Analytics Cloud has also adopted the new style of illustrations that has been introduced for the whole SAP Business Suite. They are modern, friendly, and visually appealing.

Find out more in this blog post:

  • Introducing Horizon Themes and Usability Enhancements in SAP Analytics Cloud.

 

AI-Assistance

AI can significantly enhance the user experience. In Q3/2025 we introduced these innovations where AI is embedded in the application.

AI-Assisted Commenting

If a table has a lot of comments, especially if some of them are quite lengthy, it can take effort to summarize them all succinctly. Now, with AI-assisted commenting, this is easy. It helps you compile highlights, summarizing data point comment threads, and aggregating data point comments from descendant cells. It will also translate this into your language (specified in your profile). When writing a new comment, you can use AI to help you to rephrase comments. Figure 6 below shows an example of what it looks like in Stories.

Figure 6: AI-assisted commenting, embedded in Stories. Alt Text: Screen snippet showing a report with some visible comments and a pop-over menu with the AI-entry “Summarize Comments of Descendants (English)” selected.

AI-Assisted Chart Summary

Planners and analysts often need to present to and share their findings with managers and colleagues. Summarizing charts in an easy-to-understand way is time consuming – but now much less so, thanks to AI-assisted chart summary. Generate concise, human-readable summaries of SAP Analytics Cloud charts embedded in your PowerPoint presentations at a finger tip, helping you to prepare key take aways faster and communicate more clearly. Figure 7 below shows an example, of an embedded SAP Analytics Cloud data visualization along with an AI powered summary in PowerPoint.

Figure 7: AI-assisted chart summary. Alt Text: Screenshot showing a PowerPoint screen with a chart and generated summary of it on the current slide. On the right side is the SAP Analytics Cloud panel with a preview of the chart and a button to Generate Summary.

Find out more about these, as well as AI-assisted advanced formula generation, in this blog post:

  • Sneak Peek in to SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q3 2025.

 

Personalizing your experience

For business users consuming stories and dashboards in SAP Analytics Cloud, being able to adapt the experience to suit your way of working and provide the information you need, in the way you need it, is crucial for a good user experience. I already covered some personalization above: the resizable cards in the home page, your choice of visual theme, and the other settings you can make via the user menu. However, we have introduced even more powerful personalization:

  • My Metrics, your personalized, curated collection of metrics that matter most to you and your business. 
  • Personalizing data visualization: change the way data is visualized using the new Quick Builder and save the visualization for when you return.

My Metrics

With My Metrics you can choose those metrics that matter most to you and put them together on one screen. These can come from multiple stories. In other words, rather than having to navigate to different stories to get the overview that you want, you can get a personalized overview with My Metrics. You can arrange all your metrics into categorized lists, making it easy to spot trends and understand your data at a glance.

The My Metrics Module

My Metrics was introduced as a new module within SAP Analytics Cloud, you can access it via the left panel from the new Side Menu. My Metrics is broken down into three simple areas:

  1. Your Metrics: A collection of all performance cards you have created or that have been shared with you.
  2. Detailed Visualization: When you select a metric, a chart instantly visualizes its data. The type of chart can be set during the creation of the metric. You can also see exactly which filters are currently applied.
  3. Smart Insights: Go beyond the chart with an automated analysis that reveals hidden patterns and important observations in your data.

Figure 8 shows an example:

  1. On the left, the user has selected the “Gross Margin” metric from their list of metrics.
  2. In the middle, the gross margin is visualized with a horizontal bar chart.
  3. On the right, the smart insights explain what the top contributors to the gross margin are, showing the top location contributor and the top product contributor, with a chart showing the gross margin per product.

Figure 8: My Metrics, showing the three areas described in the main text. Alt Text: Screenshot showing the three columns described in the text, each highlighted in a different color and numbered 1 to 3 from left to right.

Find out more about My Metrics in this blog post:

  • Introducing My Metrics: Your Personalized view of Metrics in SAP Analytics Cloud.

My Metrics Card on Home Page

Now that you can create your own personalized list of metrics, wouldn’t it be great if you could make them available in a card on the home page? The good news is that now you can!

Figure 9 below shows an example, where we have highlighted the My Metrics card with an additional blue border. Clicking on one of the metrics takes you directly to that metric in the My Metrics module: if you for example selected “Gross Margin”, it would take you to the screen shown in Figure 8 above.

Figure 9: The My Metrics card on the home page. Alt Text: Screenshot of the home page with three columns of cards, the rightmost card “My Sales Metrics (9/25)” spanning over the full vertical height and showing five metrics cards.

My Metrics for KPI Monitoring: Alerting

If you want to be alerted when particularly important metrics change, you can do that too: you can subscribe to metrics reports, and set the report frequency (day, week, month). You can also choose to receive reports by e-mail, or through the notification center.

Figure 10: Subscribing to a metrics report. Alt Text: Screenshot of “My Metrics” with a pop-over “Subscribe to Metrics Report” showing a guided procedure with step 1 “General Information” shown, with fields name and description; 2 “Schedule”; and 3 “Distribution List”. “Next” and “Cancel” buttons at the bottom right.

My Metrics also supports collaboration with colleagues: you can share with other users or teams and also use those that they share with you.

To sum up: you get a great user experience with my metrics, since you can personalize the experience to meet your needs: keep an eye on all the metrics that you are interested in, have them front and center on the home page, and ensure that you are notified about changes in ones that you want to track closely.

Personalizing Data Visualization

For you as a consumer of a story, if the way the data is visualized does not suit you perfectly, you can now personalize it yourself – no need to go to the folks who created the stories or dashboards, at least for common cases. The video in Figure 11 shows an example where the user wanted a different representation of the data on career levels shown in the table: with the new Quick Builder, she removed the Headcount column and added the Education Degree dimension, resulting in new rows showing the education degree for each career level.

Figure 11: Using Quick Builder to personalize data visualization. Alt Text: Animated GIF showing two charts above a table “Headcount Analysis”, and then how the user opens the Builder panel and makes the changes to the table described in the text.

 

Chart and Table Enhancements

We have invested a lot in enhancing charts and tables, providing improvement out of the box for all viewers, as well as giving those who build stories and dashboards more options to get an optimal representation. Beyond that, we have also introduced two new charts: an Area Chart and a Pareto Chart.

Table enhancements

By popular demand from customers, we have introduced a number of improvements to the user experience of tables, providing consistency with chart widgets. The enhancements include undo/redo options, mobile compatibility, expanded tooltip settings, improved data synchronization, and a new intuitive builder panel designed for customizing layouts, assigning dimensions, adding calculations, and filtering data.

Find out more in this blog post:

  • Sneak Peek in to SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q3 2025.

Chart enhancements

An enhancement available to all viewers is the new design for the Numeric Point Chart, providing more modern dashboard aesthetics and improving readability. With the fresh look, we ensure key figures stand out more prominently, making it easier for users to quickly grasp performance metrics, as shown in Figure 12.

We have also introduced a number of options for your designers to help viewers better understand the data, as shown in Figure 13 below:

  1. You can add titles to axes, so that it is clearer what data is being represented on each axis.
  2. You can add grid lines to a chart, to make it easier to interpret datapoints at a glance, or if their labels are hidden due to collision.
  3. You can choose how thick you want to make the axis line (the figure shows the “thick” option).
  4. You can choose the color and thickness of the category divider line (the figure shows the “thin” option).

Figure 13: New options for designers to optimize chart visualization. Alt Text: The left shows an example previous line chart, the right shows two versions of the new line chart, with different text orientation of the y-axis title (marked as “1”), and indicators pointing to 2 – 4 as described in the text.

Have a look at these in more detail, as well as some enhancements delivered earlier, in this blog post:

  • Chart Enhancements in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Most recently, we have enhanced the stacked area chart, so that designers can choose a 100% stacked area chart whenever they want to visualize the proportional contribution over time. Figure 14 shows an example, where the proportional contribution barely changes over time.

Figure 14: 100% stacked area chart. Alt Text: An image showing sales for each quarter in 2024, 2025 and 2026, with date on the x-axis, for other (17.6 – 18.0%), food (73.0% - 73.3%) and drink.

Find out more about the 100% stacked area chart here, as well as the new charts covered in the next section:

  • Expanding Visual Analysis: New Area Chart, Pareto Chart, and Stacked Area Enhancements.

New Charts

The new Pareto chart and Area chart help to explain trends, highlight drivers, and support prioritization.

New Pareto Chart

The Pareto Chart is designed to help Story Designers to prioritize focus, explain trade-offs, and guide action instead of treating all contributors as equally important. The chart gets its name from the Pareto principle, which states that often roughly 80% of the outcome comes from 20% of the causes – the “80/20 rule”.

It is very easy for story designers to use: We automatically calculate the Pareto visualization for you based on selected primary measure or account and the dimension. The chart is sorted from highest to lowest, including cumulative percentage which increases to 100%.

Figure 15 shows an example of building a Pareto chart, where the builder has chosen the Account “Days Order to Shipment” and the Dimension “Reasons for Delay”.
